items.scenario: You are a regular volunteer at your neighborhood community library. The branch recently cut its Saturday and Sunday opening hours, and several volunteers can no longer cover the shifts they had committed to. You want to write to the volunteer coordinator about the change.
items.direction: Write an email to the volunteer coordinator. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe how the new weekend hours affect your shifts
Explain why this creates problems for the volunteer team
Ask whether the previous schedule can be partly restored
items.subject: Weekend hours and our volunteer shift schedule

items.scenario: You recently started a part-time job at a campus bookstore. During your first shift you discovered that the inventory system you were trained on is different from the one actually in use, leaving you unsure how to process customer returns. You want to write to your supervisor.
items.direction: Write an email to your supervisor. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe the difference between the training system and the live one
Explain which task you are currently unable to complete
Ask for guidance on handling customer returns
items.subject: Question about processing returns at the register

items.scenario: You are a resident in a university apartment complex. The laundry room on your floor has been out of service for two weeks, and the nearest working machines are in a building all the way across campus. You want to write to the building manager.
items.direction: Write an email to the building manager. In your email, do the following:
goals: Describe how long the laundry room has been broken
Explain the difficulty this creates for residents on your floor
Request a clear timeline for the repair
items.subject: Out-of-service laundry room on the third floor

items.scenario: Your friend lent you a camera for a weekend photography trip. When you got home, you noticed that the lens cap was missing and the zoom ring now sticks slightly when you turn it. You want to write to your friend before returning the camera.
items.direction: Write an email to your friend. In your email, do the following:
goals: Explain what happened to the camera during the trip
Offer to repair or replace the damaged parts
Ask how your friend would prefer to handle it
items.subject: About your camera before I bring it back
